summary:
Nitrous oxide cannot be used alone as a general anesthetic because it lacks oxygen, leading to rapid development of hypoxemia and inadequate tissue perfusion. Despite having acceptable analgesic properties, its failure to provide supplemental oxygen makes it unsuitable as a sole anesthetic agent.  

$\boxed{\text{A}}$

$\boxed{\text{A}}$. Answer choice: A
